content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 20, 1989


Rodger E. Overholser
Deputy District Attorney
County of San Diego
101 W. Broadway, Suite 700
San Diego, CA 92101-8208

Re: Letter No. 89-366

Dear Mr. Overholser:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on June 16, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Jeanette Turvill, in the Legal Division, directly
at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, we will contact you shortly to advise you
as to the information needed. If your request is for informal
assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See
Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

OFFICE OF
RICHARD J. NEELY
ASSISTANT DISTRICT ATTORNEY

BRIAN E. MICHAELS
CHIEF DEPUTY DISTRICT ATTORNEY

WAYNE A. BURGESS
CHIEF INVESTIGATOR

THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY
COUNTY OF SAN DIEGO
EDWIN L. MILLER JR.
DISTRICT ATTORNEY

101 W. BROADWAY, SUITE 700
SAN DIEGO, CA 92101-8298
(619) 531-4070

June 13, 1989


Jeanette E. Turvill
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Jeanette:

     Thank you for your help last week regarding my question about
section 5(a) of the Conflict of Interest Code.

     It is my understanding, based on our conversation, that should
this office adopt the standard code it would not be necessary for
all designated employees to file an "initial" statement within 30
days after the effective date of the new code, since this office
has been covered by a conflict of interest code for some time and
all designated employees have been filing as required under the
existing code.

     I would appreciate it if you could confirm this at your
earliest convenience, by letter.

     Thanks again for the help.  It was nice talking to you again.

                                        Yours truly,

                                        [signature]
                                        RODGER E. OVERHOLSER
                                        Deputy District Attorney
                                        Special Operations Division

REO:jg

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a district attorney's office adopting a standard Conflict of Interest Code must require all designated employees to file initial statements within 30 days if the office has been covered by a conflict of interest code for some time.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC General Counsel's letter acknowledges receipt of the advice request and indicates that a formal written response should be provided within 21 working days, unless the request involves complex legal questions or requires additional information.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of incoming request letter from San Diego District Attorney dated June 13, 1989, followed by FPPC acknowledgment letter dated June 20, 1989. The actual substantive FPPC response/advice is not included in this document - only the receipt acknowledgment. Question and conclusion details are inferred from the incoming request letter rather than a formal advice letter response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a district attorney's office adopting a standard Conflict of Interest Code must require all designated employees to file initial statements within 30 days if the office has been covered by a conflict of interest code for some time.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC General Counsel's letter acknowledges receipt of the advice request and indicates that a formal written response should be provided within 21 working days, unless the request involves complex legal questions or requires additional information.
